search_queries_used: site:entnet.org cerumen impaction guideline recurrence outcomes modifying factors 2017
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic irrigation, microsuction, cerumenolytic, procedure, or referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Recurrence and impaction require direct clinician assessment.
No treatment, procedure, product, referral, or interval is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People aged 6 months and older with clinician-assessed cerumen; hearing-aid users, children, diabetes, immunocompromise, prior radiation, canal stenosis, exostoses, or non-intact tympanic membrane need individualized assessment.
setting_applicability: Primary, audiology, or ENT cerumen review.
UAE_applicability: International specialty guidance requires UAE procedure, infection-control, audiology, prescribing, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: The official AAO-HNSF 2017 cerumen guideline exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2017 update remains the current AAO-HNSF cerumen guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact cerumen scope section supports interval symptom context, functional impact, hearing or discomfort features, and relevant negatives without asserting recurrent impaction.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports cerumen-impaction identity, hearing loss, tinnitus, fullness, pain, discharge, otoscopy, ear-canal or tympanic-membrane visibility, and audiologic-test obstruction context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact modifying-factor section supports clinician-performed ENT examination and review of hearing or tympanometry information when available without authorising removal.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports diagnosis only when clinician-observed cerumen is symptomatic or prevents assessment, plus history or examination of management-modifying factors.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact outcomes section supports patient concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up, and questions without selecting treatment or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting resolution after clinician treatment, considering alternate causes when symptoms persist after resolution, and clinician referral after unsuccessful initial management without mapping a procedure or treatment instruction.

unresolved_source_gaps: Recurrence count, laterality, hearing, pain, discharge, hearing aid, prior procedure, canal and membrane findings, diagnosis, removal method, outcome, referral, and prevention remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
